metrics.narrative: The CRM's MEDDPICC pain/metrics narrative (dated 2026-03-23) frames Cell C's business problem in financial-turnaround terms: post-insolvency margin pressure, capex-light/MVNO vendor dependency, tail-spend and maverick-spend leakage, and JSE-listing/IPO audit-readiness requirements. These are the deal owner's synthesized read of Cell C's situation rather than a number voiced directly by the buyer, and no quantified ROI or business case has been confirmed by Cell C.

champion.narrative: The EY South Africa team is the primary driver of the process ('EY will front', Next_Step__c 2026-03-02) and is actively fronting the RFP, but as an external partner it is not a true internal champion. Zeyn Khan (Senior Manager, Strategic Sourcing) and Zuko Ndima (Contracts Specialist) are noted as developing internal champions who responded positively to the Feb 2026 demo, but neither has independently advanced the deal or been in confirmed direct contact since.

competition.narrative: Coupa withdrew from the evaluation on 2026-03-30, per the deal owner's Next Step note ('Coupa pulled out of this one - we have a fair chance'). No other competitor is corroborated by Competitors__c, Next_Step__c, or a buyer quote as of this sweep; a further vendor named only in the CRM's stale MEDDPICC entry (2026-03-23) could not be corroborated by any live source and is treated as unconfirmed.

identify_pain.narrative: Cell C's evaluation team responded positively to the 2026-02-16/2026-02-24 Agentic-AI-powered demo ('Cell C liked our solution', Next_Step__c), consistent with the pain themes recorded in the CRM's MEDDPICC narrative (manual/fragmented procurement, spend-visibility gaps, supplier risk, IPO-readiness pressure, 2026-03-23). The pain is well-articulated on the CRM side but not confirmed in the buyer's own words in any call transcript read this sweep; the one Avoma touchpoint (2026-05-08) was not recorded.

paper_process.narrative: No paper-process artifacts (MSA, SOW, Order Form) are on record. The deal remains in Formal Evaluation with EY still adjudicating the RFP outcome, so contracting has not begun. This is expected at this stage rather than itself a red flag, but there is no defined mechanism yet for how the deal would move to signature if Zycus is selected.

economic_buyer.narrative: Shaheen De Vries (Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement) is Cell C's named economic buyer per her Contact the relevant stakeholder attended the RFP orals and demo on 2026-05-08 (Avoma attendee record), which is her only confirmed direct engagement on file; there has been no confirmed direct contact with her, or read on her sentiment, since. Zycus is relying on the EY South Africa team to relay her position, which is a real risk given the elapsed decision window.

decision_process.narrative: A clear multi-step sequence is on record: short intro as part of a multi-vendor presentation (2026-01-27) -> demo and follow-up, with Cell C responding positively (2026-02-16 to 2026-02-24) -> EY confirms it will front the process (2026-03-02) -> RFP issued, deadline 2026-03-16, with Zycus support -> Coupa withdraws (2026-03-30) -> orals and demo delivered to EY and Cell C's evaluation team (2026-05-08), with EY forecasting a preferred-vendor appointment within a month -> extra questions answered (2026-05-29). That one-month window elapsed around 2026-06-08 with no announcement, and a status request from the deal owner on 2026-07-01 has gone unanswered as of the 2026-07-08 log entry.

sources: RFP 2026-005 subject line, Avoma manifest touchpoint 2026-05-08
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation runs through EY's RFP 2026-005 for a Contract Lifecycle Management Solution. Cell C/EY posed additional evaluation questions after the 8 May 2026 orals and demo, which the deal owner answered by 2026-05-29. Beyond the RFP's CLM-solution framing, no detailed scoring criteria or weighting has been shared with Zycus.

day_summary.overall: The only thing that happened today was a quiet pushback of the close date by a month, from end of July to end of August — no calls, emails, or notes accompanying it. That's a signal the deal isn't tracking to plan, though without any other activity logged, we can't tell whether it's a buyer-driven delay or just an internal reforecast. Ball is effectively in our court to find out why and re-engage.

cro_panel.intro: Cell C S2P is a $150,000 Formal Evaluation deal running through an EY South Africa-fronted multi-vendor RFP (2026-005, Contract Lifecycle Management Solution).; Zycus cleared an early hurdle when Coupa pulled out of the field (30 Mar 2026), delivered orals and demo to EY and Cell C's evaluation team on 8 May 2026, and answered follow-up evaluation questions by 29 May 2026.; EY had forecast a preferred-vendor decision within a month of the 8 May orals (roughly by 8 Jun 2026); that window elapsed about a month ago with no announcement, the buyer's last confirmed reply was 27 May 2026 (43 days ago), and the deal owner's 1 Jul 2026 follow-up to EY has gone unanswered as of the 8 Jul 2026 log entry.; Close date is 31 Aug 2026 (53 days out), which is tight given the process has not yet reached a confirmed shortlist/preference signal

flags: Multi-thread inside Cell C beyond EY: Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct engagement from internal Cell C stakeholders (Zeyn Khan, Zuko Ndima, Shaheen De Vries all confirmed in roles but engagement strength unclear). Risk: if EY's influence is weaker than assumed or an internal Cell C exec objects, the deal stalls. deal_scores_evidence.summary: Cell C S2P is a $150,000 Formal Evaluation deal running through an EY South Africa-fronted multi-vendor RFP (2026-005, Contract Lifecycle Management Solution). Zycus cleared an early hurdle when Coupa pulled out of the field (30 Mar 2026), delivered orals and demo to EY and Cell C's evaluation team on 8 May 2026, and answered follow-up evaluation questions by 29 May 2026. EY had forecast a preferred-vendor decision within a month of the 8 May orals (roughly by 8 Jun 2026); that window elapsed about a month ago with no announcement, the buyer's last confirmed reply was 27 May 2026 (43 days ago), and the deal owner's 1 Jul 2026 follow-up to EY has gone unanswered as of the 8 Jul 2026 log entry. Close date is 31 Aug 2026 (53 days out), which is tight given the process has not yet reached a confirmed shortlist/preference signal.

deal_risk.text: Close date of 31 Aug 2026 (53 days out) is at risk — the buyer has not yet reached a confirmed shortlist/preference decision, and EY's own decision window is already about a month overdue.

Single external channel dependency — Zycus's primary visibility into Cell C's decision runs through the EY South Africa team, with no confirmed strong internal champion advancing the deal independently.

No quantified business case on record — Cell C's post-insolvency, cost-sensitive context (per MEDDPICC, 23 Mar 2026) has not been matched with a quantified ROI case, which could weaken the deal's position if budget scrutiny increases.

win_position.text: Field narrowed in our favor — Coupa pulled out of the evaluation on 30 Mar 2026, with the deal owner noting 'we have a fair chance' (Next Step, 30/03/2026).
Early buyer reception was positive — Cell C 'liked our solution' after the Agentic AI-powered demo on 16-24 Feb 2026 (Next Step).

Decision window has elapsed — EY forecast a preferred-vendor appointment within a month of the 8 May 2026 orals (Next Step, 08/05/2026), and that window is now roughly a month overdue with no announcement.

No confirmed direct economic-buyer engagement since the orals — Shaheen De Vries attended the 8 May 2026 session (Avoma attendee record) but has had no confirmed direct contact or sentiment recorded since; Zycus is relying on EY to relay her position.

Champion is still developing, not confirmed strong — Zeyn Khan and Zuko Ndima are noted as potential internal champions (MEDDPICC, 23 Mar 2026) but neither has independently advanced the deal since the Feb 2026 demo.

deal_momentum.text: Buyer has been silent for 43 days — the last confirmed two-way touch was an incoming email reply on 27 May 2026; nothing since.
Rep outreach is going unanswered — Caroline followed up with EY on 1 Jul 2026 asking for a status update, and the 8 Jul 2026 Next Step log confirms 'no update received from EY,' 8 days on.
Deliverables were landing on schedule through late May — the deal owner answered Cell C/EY's extra evaluation questions on 29 May 2026, the last confirmed forward step.

The RFP process's own timeline has now been broken — EY's self-set one-month decision window (from 8 May 2026) elapsed around 8 Jun 2026 in silence, so continued quiet reads as a genuine stall, not normal RFP cadence.

customer_commitment.text: Cell C's evaluation team engaged directly in the 8 May 2026 orals and demo, per the Avoma attendee record, alongside the full EY South Africa team.
No dated future meeting or next milestone is currently on record beyond awaiting EY's decision — no forward commitment has been made by the buyer since 27 May 2026.
